evals.prompt: I want to add Dynamic Client Registration to my Duende IdentityServer application. Show me the full setup including the NuGet package, service registration, and endpoint mapping. I already have IdentityServer configured.
evals.expected_output: Code that adds the Duende.IdentityServer.Configuration NuGet package, calls AddIdentityServerConfiguration() on the service collection, and maps the DCR endpoint with MapDynamicClientRegistration(). Should mention the Business Edition license requirement.
assertions: Adds or references the Duende.IdentityServer.Configuration NuGet package
Calls AddIdentityServerConfiguration() on the service collection
Calls MapDynamicClientRegistration() to map the DCR endpoint
Mentions that DCR requires Business Edition or higher license
The MapDynamicClientRegistration() call includes RequireAuthorization() or the response warns about securing the endpoint

evals.prompt: How do I secure the /connect/dcr endpoint so only authorized callers can register new clients? I want to use JWT bearer authentication with a scope check.
evals.expected_output: Code that configures JWT bearer authentication for the DCR endpoint with an authorization policy requiring a specific scope, then applies RequireAuthorization to MapDynamicClientRegistration().
assertions: Configures AddJwtBearer with an Authority and Audience for the DCR scheme
Creates an authorization policy that requires an authenticated user and a specific scope claim
Applies RequireAuthorization with the named policy to MapDynamicClientRegistration()
Uses AddAuthentication() and AddAuthorization() with proper policy configuration
Does NOT expose MapDynamicClientRegistration() without authorization

evals.prompt: I need to customize DCR validation so that only authorization_code grant type is allowed, all redirect URIs must use HTTPS, and PKCE is always required. Write a custom validator.
evals.expected_output: A class extending DynamicClientRegistrationValidator that overrides ValidateGrantTypesAsync, ValidateRedirectUrisAsync, and SetClientDefaultsAsync. Registered via AddDynamicClientRegistrationValidator<T>().
assertions: Creates a class that extends DynamicClientRegistrationValidator
Overrides ValidateGrantTypesAsync to reject grant types other than authorization_code
Overrides ValidateRedirectUrisAsync to enforce HTTPS on all redirect URIs
Sets RequirePkce = true in SetClientDefaultsAsync or equivalent override
Registers the validator using AddDynamicClientRegistrationValidator<T>() on the configuration builder

evals.prompt: We need to validate software statements in our DCR flow. Software statements are signed JWTs from https://trusted-issuer.example.com. If no software statement is provided, reject the request. Apply the software_name claim from the statement to the client.
evals.expected_output: A custom DynamicClientRegistrationValidator that overrides ValidateSoftwareStatementAsync, validates the JWT against the trusted issuer, rejects requests without a software statement, and maps claims to client properties.
assertions: Overrides ValidateSoftwareStatementAsync in a DynamicClientRegistrationValidator subclass
Calls context.SetError when the software statement is missing or invalid
Validates the JWT software statement using JsonWebTokenHandler or equivalent with ValidIssuer set to the trusted issuer URL
Maps claims from the software statement (e.g. software_name) to context.Client properties
Calls base.ValidateSoftwareStatementAsync at the end of successful validation

evals.prompt: We're going to production and can't use in-memory stores for dynamically registered clients. Show me how to set up the Entity Framework-backed client configuration store for DCR.
evals.expected_output: Code that adds the Duende.IdentityServer.Configuration.EntityFramework NuGet package and calls AddClientConfigurationStore() on the configuration builder. Should warn about not using in-memory stores in production.
assertions: Adds or references the Duende.IdentityServer.Configuration.EntityFramework NuGet package
Calls AddClientConfigurationStore() on the IdentityServer configuration builder
Warns against using in-memory stores for dynamically registered clients in production
Does NOT suggest implementing IClientConfigurationStore from scratch when the EF package is available

evals.prompt: I want to implement a custom IClientConfigurationStore backed by MongoDB instead of SQL Server for storing dynamically registered clients. Show me the interface and a skeleton implementation.
evals.expected_output: A class implementing IClientConfigurationStore with all four methods: AddAsync, FindByClientIdAsync, UpdateAsync, and DeleteAsync, using MongoDB as the backing store. Registered in DI.
assertions: Implements the IClientConfigurationStore interface
Includes an AddAsync method for persisting new clients
Includes a FindByClientIdAsync method that returns Client or null
Includes UpdateAsync and DeleteAsync methods for client lifecycle management
Mentions that client secrets should be stored securely (hashed, not plaintext)

evals.prompt: We want to host DCR in a separate application from our main IdentityServer. Our IdentityServer runs at https://identity.example.com. Show me how to set up the separate DCR host.
evals.expected_output: A Program.cs for a separate application that calls AddIdentityServerConfiguration with IdentityServerBaseUrl pointed at the main IdentityServer, sets up authentication, and maps the DCR endpoint with authorization.
assertions: Calls AddIdentityServerConfiguration with options.IdentityServerBaseUrl set to 'https://identity.example.com'
Sets up JWT bearer authentication pointing to the main IdentityServer as Authority
Calls MapDynamicClientRegistration() with RequireAuthorization()
Does NOT call AddIdentityServer() — this is a separate host, not an IdentityServer instance
Mentions that the IClientConfigurationStore must be backed by the same database IdentityServer reads from

evals.prompt: What are the common security mistakes people make when setting up DCR? I want to make sure our implementation is hardened.
evals.expected_output: A comprehensive list of DCR anti-patterns and pitfalls covering unauthenticated endpoints, unrestricted grant types, in-memory stores in production, plaintext secret storage, and software statement trust.
assertions: Warns against exposing the DCR endpoint without authentication or authorization
Warns against allowing dynamically registered clients to use any grant type — should restrict and enforce PKCE
Warns against using in-memory stores for DCR clients in production
Mentions that client secrets must be stored securely (hashed, not plaintext)
Mentions that software statements must be validated against trusted signing keys — never accept unknown issuers
